# Subgrid

<FeatureLinks mdn="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_grid_layout/Subgrid" caniuse="https://caniuse.com/?search=grid-template-columns" status="Grid Level 2" label="Reference links" />

## Plain-language explanation

Let nested grids inherit parent tracks to align deep content.

## Representative syntax

```css
grid-template-columns: subgrid;
```

## Demo

<CssDemo title="Subgrid" css-b64="" code-b64="" html-b64="" caption="The demo places the layout feature in a realistic UI fragment where primary content and supporting metrics need stable alignment." lang="en-US" badge="Practical demo" />

## When to use it

<div class="usage-grid">
<section>
  <h3>Best fit</h3>
  <p>Use Subgrid in complex layout when you need to express alignment, wrapping, and order as layout rules instead of extra wrapper markup. It usually gives the stylesheet a clearer job than extra classes, wrappers, or script.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Example</h3>
  <p>A settings page can align every nested control row while a responsive gallery chooses the right packing behavior.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Watch out</h3>
  <p>Layout features can change reading and focus order indirectly. Check the experience with keyboard navigation, not only screenshots.</p>
</section>
</div>

## Source metadata

- Category: Layout, Positioning, and Reading Order
- Maturity / source: Grid Level 2
- Representative syntax: `grid-template-columns: subgrid;`
- Position in category: 2 / 16
